questions.question:
Gateway API resources in namespace `gateway-lab` are inconsistent.

A broken `Gateway` named `main-gateway`, a broken `HTTPRoute` named `app-routes`, and backend workloads already exist. Repair the Gateway API configuration so that:
- a `GatewayClass` named `cka-014-gc` exists with controller name `example.com/gateway-controller`
- `Gateway` `main-gateway` uses GatewayClass `cka-014-gc`
- the Gateway listens on HTTP port `80`
- `HTTPRoute` `app-routes` attaches to `main-gateway`
- host `apps.example.local` routes `/app1` to service `app1-svc` port `8080`
- host `apps.example.local` routes `/app2` to service `app2-svc` port `8080`
- any stale `/legacy` route is removed
- save the repaired HTTPRoute manifest to `/tmp/exam/q401/app-routes.yaml`

Do not recreate the backend Deployments or Services unless it is strictly required.

verification.description: GatewayClass and Gateway use the intended listener and class contract
HTTPRoute attaches to the Gateway and routes /app1 and /app2 to the intended backends
Backend services remain ready and the repaired HTTPRoute manifest is exported

questions.question:
A multi-container Deployment named `ops-api` in namespace `triage-lab` is unhealthy. The `api` container should serve nginx on port `80`, and the `log-agent` sidecar should tail `/var/log/ops/app.log`, but both containers are misconfigured.

Use workload logs and pod resource output to triage the issue, then repair the Deployment so that:
- the `api` container uses `containerPort` `80`
- the `api` container memory limit is `256Mi`
- the `api` container liveness probe checks port `80`
- the `log-agent` container uses env `LOG_TARGET=/var/log/ops/app.log`
- save the previous logs from container `log-agent` to `/tmp/exam/q402/log-agent-previous.log`
- save `kubectl top pod <active-pod> --containers` output to `/tmp/exam/q402/ops-api-top.txt`

Do not recreate the Deployment unless it is strictly required.

verification.description: The Deployment uses the intended port, memory limit, liveness probe, and sidecar log target
Previous log evidence and pod resource usage output are exported to the expected files
The repaired multi-container workload becomes Available without ongoing sidecar restarts
